title: Whats the difference between HIP and Seele
display_title: Whats the difference between HIP and Seele
wikitext:
[]: What's the difference between HIP and Seele?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Wed Oct 14, 2009 12:58 pm ...between the Human Instrumentality committee and SEELE?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Oct 14, 2009 1:17 pm All members of the committee are in Seele, but not all members of Seele are in the committee. Also, the committee is the public entity that Nerv works with that is backed by Seele, which Nerv is not familiar with, with the exception of a few key players. One is a public front, the other is a secret society.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Wed Oct 14, 2009 1:20 pm It is sort of like the relationship between the (actual, real world) United Nations and the Security Council. There are five permanent members of the Security Council, but they are also UN members, but not all UN members get to be on the permanent Security Council. The Committee is the "inner circle". That confused me at first, as well.

[]: Re: What's the difference...             Quote Post by DIDDY » Fri Oct 16, 2009 11:35 pm CyberXIII wrote: ...between the Human Instrumentality committee and SEELE?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Oct 19, 2009 3:48 pm What EvaMonkey said. Also, CyberXIII, please refrain from making unclear topics unless it invovles spoilers.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Mon Oct 19, 2009 5:56 pm Oh dear, you used the red "this means business" text color. Okay, okay, I won't do that here. No need to get testy.

Article: Whats the difference between HIP and Seele
[]: What's the difference between HIP and Seele?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Wed Oct 14, 2009 12:58 pm ...between the Human Instrumentality committee and SEELE?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Oct 14, 2009 1:17 pm All members of the committee are in Seele, but not all members of Seele are in the committee. Also, the committee is the public entity that Nerv works with that is backed by Seele, which Nerv is not familiar with, with the exception of a few key players. One is a public front, the other is a secret society.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Wed Oct 14, 2009 1:20 pm It is sort of like the relationship between the (actual, real world) United Nations and the Security Council. There are five permanent members of the Security Council, but they are also UN members, but not all UN members get to be on the permanent Security Council. The Committee is the "inner circle". That confused me at first, as well.
